title: Unmanaged or Orphaned Device Detection
dataSources: Primary: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:site:topology`. from the `site_topology` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per site per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `siteId` (string), `siteName` (string), `siteType` (string), `parentSiteName` (string). CIM data model: Compute_Inventory. Secondary: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:devicehealth`. from the `devicehealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per managed device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `overallHealth` (integer 0-100), `reachabilityHealth` (string), `deviceName` (string), `deviceType` (string), `siteId` (string), `platformId` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `softwareVersion` (string). Correlation: join on `siteId` to identify devices not assigned to any site in the topology hierarchy.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site:topology" | stats dc(deviceId) as assigned_devices by siteId, siteName | append [search index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" | stats dc(deviceName) as total_devices] | eval unassigned=total_devices-assigned_devices | where unassigned > 0 | table siteName assigned_devices total_devices unassigned
description: Identifies network devices that are managed by Catalyst Center but not assigned to any site in the hierarchy, indicating incomplete provisioning.
value: Orphaned devices miss site-specific policies, compliance checks, and reporting groupings. Finding them ensures complete coverage.
implementation: Enable the `site_topology` input for site-to-device assignments and the `devicehealth` input for the full managed device list, both targeting `index=catalyst`. Align field names (`deviceId` vs `deviceName`) with your TA extractions; adjust the search if your deployment uses different join keys.
visualization: Table (siteName, assigned_devices, total_devices, unassigned), optional follow-on search listing unassigned device names from device health records only.
knownFalsePositives:
**Newly discovered device not yet assigned to a site in the hierarchy.** Catalyst Center may discover a device through CDP/LLDP or manual addition, but the administrator has not yet placed it in the site hierarchy. The device is managed but appears orphaned. Distinguish by checking `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device"` for the device's `hostname` and verifying it exists in inventory but has no `siteId` in `site:topology`. Suppress by allowing a 72-hour grace period for newly discovered devices before alerting on missing site assignment.

**Device identifier mismatch between topology and devicehealth sourcetypes.** The subsearch compares `deviceId` from `site:topology` with `deviceName` from `devicehealth`. If these identifiers do not align (UUID vs hostname), legitimate devices will appear unmatched. Distinguish by verifying the join key: ensure the SPL uses a consistent identifier (e.g., `managementIpAddress`) that appears in both sourcetypes. This is a query design issue, not a false positive — fix the SPL join key if identifiers are mismatched.

**Site hierarchy restructuring temporarily orphaning devices.** When sites are moved, merged, or renamed in Catalyst Center > Design > Network Hierarchy, devices may temporarily lose their site assignment until the new hierarchy propagates. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"` for site hierarchy changes. Suppress by requiring orphaned status to persist for 2+ polls (2+ hours) before alerting.

**Virtual domain scope showing partial inventory.** If the TA service account has access to only a subset of virtual domains, the topology and device health data may cover different device sets, creating apparent mismatches. Distinguish by comparing `| stats dc(deviceId)` across both sourcetypes and checking whether the gap corresponds to devices in a restricted virtual domain. This is a configuration gap — expand virtual domain access or accept the scope limitation.

references.title: Catalyst Center Device Health API — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (7538) with **`site_topology`** (or equivalent) to `cisco:dnac:site:topology` and **`devicehealth`** to `cisco:dnac:devicehealth`, both landing in `index=catalyst`.
• Agree on a single stable device key across streams (`deviceId` from topology vs `deviceName` in health); normalize with `eval` or **props** if the TA uses different field names on each sourcetype.
• `docs/implementation-guide.md` for index routing and input hosts.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• **Intent API:** site topology and device health are separate TA modular inputs; confirm poll intervals and that both are **enabled** with no `ERROR` in `splunkd.log` on the input host.
• **Catalyst Center** must assign devices to sites for topology events to include membership; unassigned management gear may always appear in health but not in site rolls.

Step 2 — Create the search

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site:topology" | stats dc(deviceId) as assigned_devices by siteId, siteName | append [search index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" | stats dc(deviceName) as total_devices] | eval unassigned=total_devices-assigned_devices | where unassigned > 0 | table siteName assigned_devices total_devices unassigned
```

Understanding this SPL
• This is a **heuristic** gap check: the subsearch is a **fleet-wide** `dc` while the main branch is **per site**; production often replaces `append` with a **summary** or **join** of actual unassigned device lists from a dedicated inventory field in Catalyst Center exports.
• Tune `unassigned>0` only after you prove `deviceId` and `deviceName` (or a unified `deviceId` on both) represent the same population.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
• `dc(deviceId)` by site; `dc(deviceName)` global from health; the difference is **not** mathematically a count of unassigned per row unless you intentionally aggregate — use this as a **signal** to open an inventory report in Catalyst, not as strict accounting without validation.

Step 3 — Validate
• Cross-check counts against **Catalyst Center > Inventory** and **site assignment** in the UI; export a list of unassigned devices from Cisco if the TA exposes that field, and align Splunk.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Dashboard table for capacity owners; runbook: assign to site, then re-poll and confirm row clears.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Inflated unassigned:** mixed identifier types; switch both sides to the same UDI or `serialNumber` if indexed.
• **No topology events:** `site_topology` input disabled, wrong site scope, or no device-to-site data for that controller domain.
• **Stagnant `total_devices`:** `devicehealth` poller throttled; compare `dc(deviceName)` in Splunk to fleet size in the UI.
grandmaExplanation: We find network devices that are being managed but are not assigned to any building or floor, which means they may be missing important settings.
